title: mercurialis × longistipes
intro: Mercurialis × longistipes, also known as the long-stalked dog's mercury, is a hybrid plant of the genus Mercurialis, created by crossing Mercurialis annua and Mercurialis perennis. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Mercurialis × longistipes include its ability to treat various conditions such as arthritis, skin infections, and urinary tract issues. Mercurialis × longistipes can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
